nodes.label: Settings Audit Lessons
nodes.description: 6 findings (CRIT-2 + IMP-5) from settings-flow.jsx audit; commits to 39f4107

nodes.label: CRIT-1: stale closure in EliminarCuentaSubScreen
nodes.description: onDeleted prop not in useEffect deps — stale closure bug. Fix: add to [step, onDeleted]
nodes.rationale: Builder-mode focus on step-machine flow missed that onDeleted is a prop not a stable value

nodes.label: CRIT-2: isPlanCancelled not reset on close
nodes.description: Permanently-mounted component (open prop) kept local state between sessions. Fix: reset in useEffect([open])
nodes.rationale: Builder-mode assumed component destructs on close; the always-mounted+open-prop pattern requires explicit state reset
nodes.norm_label: crit-2: isplancancelled not reset on close

nodes.label: IMP-7: Bridge __mtxPlayContent Settings→Explorar
nodes.description: Cross-file bridge implemented: __mtxCloseSettings in MentexApp, __mtxPlayContent in explore-flow.jsx, fallback to __explore_home__

nodes.label: Always-mounted + open prop pattern
nodes.description: Dominant pattern in Mentex: screens never unmount, receive open:bool prop. Requires explicit useEffect([open]) to reset all local state on close

nodes.label: Universal links for socials (2026 standard)
nodes.description: Use https:// universal links instead of custom URL schemes (instagram://) — OS opens native app when installed, browser otherwise; no double-call or fallback timers

nodes.label: Window global store pattern
nodes.description: Stores live on window (__mtxProfile, __mtxFollows, __mtxReviews, __mtxAutoRoutines); emit CustomEvents; React hooks subscribe via addEventListener

nodes.label: ReactDOM.createPortal overlay pattern
nodes.description: Sheets that must render above the tab bar use ReactDOM.createPortal into #mtx-overlay-root — escapes stacking context of parent component

nodes.label: Seed-deterministic mock data
nodes.description: Foreign user stats derived deterministically from userId.charCodeAt(0) seed — consistent across renders without backend

nodes.label: __mtxScheduler IIFE singleton
nodes.description: IIFE-initialized global singleton (window.__mtxScheduler). Guards against double-init. Provides schedule, cancel, clearAll, getTime, list, requestPermission, _restoreTimeouts, _processReminders. Persists state in sessionStorage '__mtx_schedule_v1'.

nodes.label: __mtxScheduler.schedule(id, time, label, itemType)
nodes.description: Schedules a notification for a given id at HH:MM time with label and semantic itemType (ritual|content|focus|reminder). Clears existing timeout, creates new setTimeout, saves to sessionStorage, dispatches 'mtx:scheduled' CustomEvent, calls _bridgeUpsert, then calls __mtxIAAgenda.checkConflict.

nodes.label: __mtxScheduler.cancel(id)
nodes.description: Cancels a scheduled item: clears timeout, removes from _items, saves sessionStorage, dispatches 'mtx:scheduled', calls _bridgeRemove to remove from agenda.

nodes.label: __mtxScheduler.clearAll()
nodes.description: Clears all scheduled items: iterates _items, clears timeouts and bridge-removes each, resets _items to {}, saves sessionStorage, dispatches 'mtx:scheduled' with id=null.

nodes.label: __mtxScheduler._restoreTimeouts()
nodes.description: Re-arms setTimeout for every item loaded from sessionStorage on page load. Also calls _bridgeUpsertLazy for each restored item to re-sync with __mtxIAAgenda.

nodes.label: __mtxScheduler._processReminders()
nodes.description: Reads __mtxIAAgenda.get().reminders array, schedules any incomplete reminders that are not yet tracked in _items. Called by SchedulerInit on mount and on every 'mtx:ia-agenda-changed' event.

nodes.label: itemType param (ritual|content|focus|reminder)
nodes.description: 4th parameter to schedule(). Semantic color discriminator for the AgendaSheet timeline. Valid values: 'ritual', 'content', 'focus', 'reminder'. Defaults to 'focus' when undefined. Persisted in _items[id].itemType and forwarded to __mtxIAAgenda.upsertMentexEvent as 'type'.

nodes.label: sessionStorage '__mtx_schedule_v1' persistence
nodes.description: Ephemeral per-tab persistence key. Stores { [id]: { time, label } } — tid is omitted on save. Loaded on IIFE init. Written on every schedule/cancel/clearAll/_processReminders call.

nodes.label: _bridgeUpsert(id, time, label, itemType)
nodes.description: Synchronously calls __mtxIAAgenda.upsertMentexEvent with id prefixed 'sched_', source='mentex', durationMin=30. Falls through silently if __mtxIAAgenda not yet available.

nodes.label: _bridgeUpsertLazy(id, time, label, itemType)
nodes.description: Polls for __mtxIAAgenda availability (150ms interval, max 20 attempts ~3s) to handle race conditions on page reload before calling _bridgeUpsert. Used by _restoreTimeouts.

nodes.label: _bridgeRemove(id)
nodes.description: Calls __mtxIAAgenda.removeMentexEvent('sched_' + id). Falls through silently if agenda not available.

nodes.label: useScheduledTime(id) hook
nodes.description: React hook returning the scheduled time string for a given id (or null). Subscribes to 'mtx:scheduled' CustomEvent to stay in sync. Re-reads from __mtxScheduler.getTime when id matches or detail.id === null (clearAll).

nodes.label: SchedulerInit component
nodes.description: Render-null React component. On mount: calls requestPermission() and _processReminders(). Attaches window listener for 'mtx:ia-agenda-changed' to re-run _processReminders on every agenda update. Returns null.

nodes.label: Notification API + __mtxToast fallback
nodes.description: Fires browser Notification when permission='granted'. Falls back to window.__mtxToast.show() with 7000ms duration. Used by _fireNotification called from each setTimeout callback.

nodes.label: CustomEvent 'mtx:scheduled'
nodes.description: Dispatched on document after every schedule/cancel/clearAll. Payload: { detail: { id } } where id=null for clearAll. Consumed by useScheduledTime hook.

nodes.label: window event 'mtx:ia-agenda-changed'
nodes.description: Consumed (not produced) by SchedulerInit. Triggers _processReminders() on every agenda mutation to sync reminder items into the scheduler.

nodes.label: __mtxIAAgenda (external singleton reference)
nodes.description: External window global consumed by mtx-scheduler. Methods used: upsertMentexEvent, removeMentexEvent, checkConflict, get(). Not defined in this file.

nodes.label: MtxHeader component
nodes.description: App-level header with time-based greeting (Buenos días/tardes/noches), dynamic subtitle, and notification bell button with neon badge counter. Props: name, subtitle, notifCount, onNotif.

nodes.label: Sparkline component

nodes.label: MtxSectionHead component
nodes.description: Section header used across all screen blocks. Shows h2 title with optional eyebrow/subtitle and right-side action (text link or icon button). Props: title, action, eyebrow, subtitle, onAction, actionIcon, actionLabel, actionRadius.

nodes.label: SliderRow

nodes.label: IAToggleRow

nodes.label: AvatarPicker

nodes.label: useIAConfig()
nodes.description: Custom hook that subscribes to mtx:ia-config-changed window event via useEffect. Uses useReducer force-increment pattern for re-render. Returns snapshot from window.__mtxIAConfig on each event.

nodes.label: __mtxIAConfig
nodes.description: IIFE-initialized global store (window.__mtxIAConfig). Holds agent, memory[], knowledge{}, channels{}, integrations{} state. Emits mtx:ia-config-changed CustomEvent with deep-cloned snapshot on every mutation. Methods: snapshot, setAgentField, applyPersonalityPreset, addMemory, updateMemory, removeMemory, clearMemory, setKnowledge, setChannel, setIntegration, exportData, resetAll.

nodes.label: mtx:ia-config-changed
nodes.description: CustomEvent dispatched on window by __mtxIAConfig._emit() on every state mutation. Carries detail.snapshot (deep-clone of full state). Consumed by useIAConfig hook.

nodes.label: mtx:toast
nodes.description: CustomEvent dispatched on window by PrivacyTab actions (export, clearChats, clearMemory, resetAll). Carries detail.message and detail.duration.

nodes.label: Screen: Fix — Finalizar / Escudo Activo confirmation modal
nodes.description: Full-screen modal shown when user attempts to deactivate the shield mid-session. Background shows an animated circular countdown timer showing '6' with label 'RESPIRA' and subtitle 'ESCUDO ACTIVO'. A pill button '⊙ SOLTAR EL ESCUDO' is shown in warning style. Modal copy: '¿Soltar la mente al ruido?' with body text 'Si apagas la protección, las apps quedan libres durante el resto de la sesión.' Two CTAs: primary green button 'Mantener mi escudo' and destructive text link 'Sí, finalizar la protección'. Mentex Tweaks panel is open on the right showing: AUTH (DEV) section, Estado (Inactivo/Activo toggle), Acento color toggle (active/green), Estilo de cards (Glass/Líneas/Plano), Tipografía (Geist/Inter/Manrope).

nodes.label: Screen: Disable Confirm — blank/black screen bug
nodes.description: Completely black screen with only a faint very dark green gradient glow visible at the top center. This documents a critical rendering bug: when the user taps 'Sí, finalizar la protección' (confirm deactivation), the screen goes fully black instead of navigating to the expected post-session or home screen. This is a navigation/state bug — the shield deactivation flow does not correctly transition the UI after confirmation.
nodes.norm_label: screen: disable confirm — blank/black screen bug

nodes.label: Escudo (Shield) — Core App-Blocking Feature
nodes.description: The primary focus/protection feature of Mentex. Blocks distracting apps (Instagram, TikTok, YouTube, Spotify, Facebook) for a user-selected session duration (15/30/45 min, 1 hour). Has two states: Inactivo and Activo. When active, shows animated circular breathing timer ('RESPIRA', countdown). Deactivation requires explicit confirmation via a modal with friction copy to discourage breaking focus. Inspired by Opal-style blocking.

nodes.label: Bug: Active shield state not visually distinct on home screen
nodes.description: When the escudo transitions from inactive to active, the home screen ('Mejora tu concentración' app list + duration selector) does not update its visual appearance. Documented by comparing debug-desbloquear.png (inactive) vs debug-desbloquear-activo.png (active) — the two screenshots look nearly identical.
nodes.fix_commit_hint: debug-fix-desbloquear.png shows post-fix state with content card surfaced
nodes.norm_label: bug: active shield state not visually distinct on home screen

nodes.label: Bug: Black screen after confirming shield deactivation
nodes.description: After user taps 'Sí, finalizar la protección' on the escudo deactivation confirmation modal, the app renders a completely black screen. A faint dark green gradient glow is visible at top, suggesting the background theme color is applied but no UI components are mounted. Root cause likely: navigation failure or unmounted component after shield state reset. Documented in debug-disable-confirm.png.
nodes.norm_label: bug: black screen after confirming shield deactivation

nodes.label: Component: Escudo Deactivation Confirmation Modal
nodes.description: Full-screen overlay shown when user requests to stop the shield mid-session. Contains: animated circular breathing countdown timer, '⊙ SOLTAR EL ESCUDO' pill indicator, friction-copy headline '¿Soltar la mente al ruido?', explanatory body text, primary CTA 'Mantener mi escudo' (green), and destructive text CTA 'Sí, finalizar la protección'. Designed with deliberate friction to reduce accidental or impulsive deactivation.

nodes.label: Component: Home Screen Content Card (shield-active state)
nodes.description: When the escudo is active, the home screen header is replaced by a 'INICIO PARA EL DÍA' card surfacing recommended content (e.g. 'Hábitos Atómicos' book cover with CTAs). This is the post-fix behavior introduced to visually distinguish the active shield state and provide motivational/educational content during the session.

nodes.label: Cancellation Flow

Three exit paths: keep premium (from step 1 or offer), accept offer, or cancel anyway.

nodes.label: Cancel Reason Chip

nodes.label: Tu Progreso Card

nodes.label: Audit Finding: Cancel Reason Chip Text Truncation
nodes.description: In audit-05 (original), the 'Poco contenido de mi interés' chip had insufficient height, potentially truncating the two-line label. Fixed in audit-10: chip height expanded to properly accommodate wrapped text. Visual comparison between audit-05 and audit-10 confirms the fix.
nodes.norm_label: audit finding: cancel reason chip text truncation

nodes.label: Audit Finding: Conditional Textarea Reveal
nodes.description: Optional textarea for free-text feedback only appears after a cancellation reason is selected (confirmed by comparing audit-05 vs audit-06). This is intentional progressive disclosure. The textarea placeholder text: '¿Hay algo que podríamos mejorar para que te quedaras?'. Section label: 'CUÉNTANOS MÁS (OPCIONAL)'.

nodes.label: Audit Finding: Dynamic Retention Offer by Churn Reason
nodes.description: When user selects 'El precio es muy alto' as cancellation reason, the offer screen shows a price-specific offer (50% DESC for 3 months at $4.99/mes). This implies the retention offer is dynamically chosen based on the selected churn reason — a deliberate product decision for personalized winback.
nodes.norm_label: audit finding: dynamic retention offer by churn reason
